# Compatibility Report
- Earth2160
- Steam AppID: 1900

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT 
- Video driver version: mesa 24.1.6-1
- Kernel version: 6.10.6-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/KuczaRacza/90c8fd992be069f5742533fd33973a87):
- Proton version: Proton experimental BuildID 15306265

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
App can't connect to internet. It prevents from restringing product blocking gameplay. No crash or  freeze.
[steam-1900.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16754499/steam-1900.log)



## Reproduction
- Launch game
- Try to register after providing cd keys
- Click activate via internet
- Fill form with any data
- Connection failed (issue)

issue_title: Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare 2: Deluxe Edition (1922560)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Plants vs Zombies Garden Warfare 2
- Steam AppID of the game: 1922560

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6600
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.1.5
- Kernel version: 6.4.9-arch1-1
- Proton version: GE 8-11, Experimental, & 8.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When running the game, the Origin installer runs, however after which it keeps a blank floating window open without the game itself running. Following a file integrity check, or reinstall, the same process happens again, origin installer works, etc.

To mitigate this, I have tried following what was suggested in protondb (adding "dxgi.customVendorId = 1c06" to a file dxvk.conf).

I have run proton with logs enabled and attached them here;

[steam-1922560.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12325264/steam-1922560.log)

## Reproduction

Run the game with any version of steam.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Terra Invicta
- Steam AppID of the game: 1176470

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1070
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 510.85.02
- Kernel version: 5.15
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [info](https://gist.github.com/lupuchard/d3188925a3ee120cf896a9d747714908)
- Proton version: 7.0-4

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.
[steam-1176470.log.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9686431/steam-1176470.log.tar.gz)

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Frame rate drops to below 1 fps on the ship designer screen. Rest of the game runs fine.

## Reproduction
1. Start new game and progress until you have access to the ship designer (or load game with access to ship designer).
2. Open ship designer.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
3. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
4. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
5. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Stationeers
- Steam AppID of the game: 544550

## System Information
- GPU: GeForce GTX 860M
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 460.91.03
- Kernel version: 5.4.0-81-generic
- Link to full system information report as Gist: [System report](https://gist.github.com/MrBigss/13722a91823d0c87228bf60c5868860e)
- Proton version: 6.3-6

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[Log from PROTON_LOG=1 steam-544550.log.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7026305/steam-544550.log.zip)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

In the game, the mouse is used in two way. First, when interacting with the environment with the items, or in the menus before the game. Those are simple "you click, it does something".
When in first person, in the game, you can also hold alt, and the cursor shows up. This allows opening the inventories, and manipulating things in the inventory, without using the keyboard.
The issue is that, while the mouse shows up, and the cursor shows up, now the clicks do not seem to register anymore.
I can't pick up items, or even open/close the inventory menus.

Also tried with Proton experimental, same problem now. Experimental didn't use to work correctly for the game, hence why I was plagin on Proton 6.3.-5
There was no update to the game since the problem showed up.
Compositing has been off for all versions.



## Reproduction
Start a game, get in first person mode, and then try to manipulate the UI with the mouse. Mouse shows up, "highlighting" shows up when hovering items, but mouse clicks do not register.


<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Lorelai
- Steam AppID of the game: 593960

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Custom APU 0405
- Video driver version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 24.1.7
- Kernel version: 6.11.0-1-default
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/bjoern-tantau/f2f53b61bf57df9e3b2c2cdd756578f3
- Proton version: 9 and experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When you watch TV with Al only a testimage is shown instead of the video of the TV channel. When playing with Proton GE the game hangs indefinately.

## Reproduction

Play normally until you can mess with or help Al. On the third night you can sit on the couch with him to watch TV. If you choose the option to watch TV no actual video is played.

issue_title: METAL GEAR SOLID Δ: SNAKE EATER (2417610)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater
- Steam AppID of the game: 2417610

## System Information
- GPU: Sapphire Nitro+ RX 9070 XT
- Video driver version: 25.2.1
- Kernel version: 6.16.2
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [here](https://gist.github.com/NyaomiDEV/92a286c0f213fe684c4a2688decdb153)
- Proton version: 9.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms

<details><summary>Game wouldn't start after choosing language and graphics preset. Dmesg shows amdgpu traces leading to vkd3d being the culprit. This same behaviour was already noticed on another game (Mafia The Old Country) when launching it with FSR enabled.</summary>
